name: Flower Brain: 6-Cell Ternary Architecture (Scaled)
author: G3sparky (Gavin Saunders)
category_note: Trained on 1xH100 SXM for ~60 min. NOT eligible for 10-min 8xH100 record track.
blurb: Scaled Flower Brain: 6-cell ternary architecture, 32.5M params, 512-dim, 12 layers, depth recurrence. Pre-quant val_bpb 1.1155 (competitive with main leaderboard). Post-quant 1.80 (ternary gap 0.68). Compresses to 10.4MB (35% under 16MB budget). Trained on 1xH100 SXM in ~60 min.
compression: mixed ternary (2 bits/weight for MLP) + int6 GPTQ (attention) + brotli
key_changes: Pre-quant 1.1155 — competitive with main leaderboard entries
Depth recurrence (2 loops, layers 3-5, 17 virtual layers)
Mixed ternary + GPTQ compression: 10.4MB (35% under budget)
Finding: STE makes quantization gap WORSE (0.68 vs 0.29 without STE)

experimental_findings.conclusion: Gap B is a projection problem, not a training problem. Void fraction (16-17%) is architecture-determined. Weight decay regularizes for quantization.

base: Novel architecture — not derived from competition baseline
attribution: BitNet b1.58 (Ma et al. 2024), Flower of Life topology (Saunders 2026, AU Patent 2026902541), PNN ternary research (76.5% vs 15.3% binary, p=2.18e-11)
